value.title: Falcon 1 - Wikipedia
value.content: # Falcon 1. | Falcon 1 flight 4, the first successful launch of a privately developed, fully liquid-fueled rocket to enter orbit around the Earth. **Falcon 1** was a two-stage small-lift launch vehicle that was operated from 2006 to 2009 by SpaceX, an American aerospace manufacturer. The Falcon 1 used LOX/RP-1 for both stages, the first stage powered by a single pump-fed Merlin engine, and the second stage powered by SpaceX's pressure-fed Kestrel vacuum engine. On July 14, 2009, Falcon 1 made its second successful flight, delivering the Malaysian RazakSAT satellite to orbit on SpaceX's first commercial launch (fifth and final launch overall). While SpaceX had announced an enhanced variant, the Falcon 1e, following this flight, the Falcon 1 was retired in favor of the Falcon 9 v1.0, the first version of the company’s successful and long-running Falcon 9 launch vehicle. While the development of Falcon 1 was privately funded, the first two Falcon 1 launches were purchased by the United States Department of Defense under a program that evaluates new US launch vehicles suitable for use by DARPA.

value.content: # Falcon 1 | Flight 3. # Watch the Launch. 3rd launch of Falcon 1 (Failed) from Kwajalein Atoll 3 August 2008, 03:34 GMT Payload: Trailblazer (ORS), PRESat (NASA), NanoSail-D (NASA), Explorers (Celestis) Residual stage 1 thrust led to collisi... ### Falcon 1 - Wikipedia. Falcon 1 was a small-lift launch vehicle that was operated from 2006 to 2009[5] by SpaceX, an American aerospace manufacturer. On 28 September 2008, Falcon 1 became the first privately-developed fully liquid-fueled launch vehicle to go into orbit around the Earth.[6][7]: 203 The two-stage-to-orbit ... ### FALCON 1, FLIGHT 3 MISSION SUMMARY. # Flight 3. Trailblazer, PRESat, NanoSail-D and a Celestis payload were part of the rideshare on the 3rd Falcon 1 launch. The Falcon 1 was the first launch vehicle developed and manufactured by SpaceX in 2006. A batch of 24 satellites for the Starlink mega-constellation - SpaceX's project for space-based Internet communication system.

value.title: SpaceX revisits first successful launch to orbit with Falcon 1 rocket

SpaceX revisits first successful launch to orbit with Falcon 1 rocket
VideoFromSpace
2130000 subscribers
201 likes
9297 views
14 Nov 2023
The Falcon 1 made history on September 28, 2008 by becoming the "first privately developed liquid fuel rocket to reach Earth orbit," according to SpaceX.  Hear from the team that made it happen. 

Credit: SpaceX
